questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Which of the following statements regarding the duration of day and night is correct?
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements regarding the duration of day and night is correct?

options.a: Difference is least near the Equator and progressively increases away from it
options.b: Difference is maximum at the Equator and progressively decreases away from it
options.c: Difference is least at the Tropics and progressively increases towards the Equator and Poles
options.d: Difference is maximum at the Tropics and progressively decreases towards the Equator and Poles

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The duration of day and night is determined by the tilt of the Earth's axis and its rotation around the Sun.
Near the **Equator**, which is located at 0 degrees latitude, the **difference between day and night duration is relatively small** throughout the year.
This is because the Equator receives nearly equal amounts of sunlight year-round due to its proximity to the Sun's direct rays. As one moves **away from the Equator towards higher latitudes**, such as towards the Poles, the **difference in the duration of day and night progressively increases**.
This is because the angle at which sunlight reaches the Earth's surface becomes more oblique, resulting in shorter days and longer nights during certain times of the year.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Which one of the following statements is correct with reference to normal lapse rate?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is correct with reference to normal lapse rate?

options.a: Temperature is highest at ground level and decreases with increasing altitude.
options.b: Temperature is lowest at ground level and increases with increasing altitu.de.
options.c: Temperature remains stable with increasing altitude.
options.d: Temperature first increases with increasing altitude and gradually starts decreasing.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The normal lapse rate is the rate at which **temperature decreases with increasing altitude in the troposphere**.
The normal lapse rate is approximately **6.5C per kilometer** (18.8F per mile). This means that for every kilometer you go up in altitude, the temperature will decrease by 6.5C.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Which one of the following geologic time periods is associated with the first appearance of the marsupials?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following geologic time periods is associated with the first appearance of the marsupials?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The first known marsupial fossils date back to the **Lower Cretaceous** period, which was about 125 million years ago.
The Lower Cretaceous period occurred approximately 145 to 100 million years ago. These fossils were found in North America and Asia. Marsupials eventually spread to Australia and South America, where they diversified into a wide variety of species.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: In order to find out the absolute location of a place on the map, which of the following will be required?
questions.questionText: In order to find out the absolute location of a place on the map, which of the following will be required?

options.c: Both latitude and longitude of the place
options.d: Neither latitude nor longitude of the place

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Latitude and longitude are two sets of coordinates** that are used to determine the absolute location of a place on the map.
Latitude is the measure of a place's distance north or south of the equator, while longitude is the measure of a place's distance east or west of the Prime Meridian.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Which one of the following represents an inner planet?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following represents an inner planet?

options.a: Planets between the Sun and the Earth
options.b: Planets between the Sun and the belt of asteroids

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. Inner planets refer to the planets that are located **between the Sun and the asteroid belt**.
These planets include **Mercury, Venus, Earth, and Mars**. They are called inner planets because they are closer to the Sun compared to the outer planets (Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune) which are located beyond the asteroid belt.
**Important Tips** The inner and outer planets are two groups of planets in our solar system. They have different characteristics and locations relative to the sun. Here are some of the main differences between them:
The **inner planets are Mercury, Venus, Earth, and Mars**. The outer planets are Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune. The **inner planets are closer to the sun and have shorter orbits**.
The outer planets are farther away and have longer orbits. The **inner planets are smaller and rockier**. They are also called terrestrial planets because they have solid surfaces.
The outer planets are larger and gassier. They are also called gas giants because they are made mostly of hydrogen and helium. The **inner planets have either no moons or few moons**. Earth has one moon, Mars has two, and Mercury and Venus have none. The outer planets have numerous moons.
Jupiter has 79, Saturn has 82, Uranus has 27, and Neptune has 143. The **inner planets have no rings**. The outer planets have rings made of dust, ice, and rocks.
Saturn's rings are the most visible and complex.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: If the equatorial plane of Earth is not inclined to its orbit, then
questions.questionText: If the equatorial plane of Earth is not inclined to its orbit, then

options.a: Duration of the year would be longer
options.c: There would be no change of seasons

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. Earth's tilt causes the seasons because it affects how much sunlight each hemisphere receives throughout the year.
When one hemisphere is tilted toward the Sun, it receives more direct and intense rays, making it warmer and longer in daylight hours. This is summer for that hemisphere. When the same hemisphere is tilted away from the Sun, it receives less direct and weaker rays, making it colder and shorter in daylight hours.
This is winter for that hemisphere. If the **equatorial plane of Earth** (the plane perpendicular to its axis) was **not inclined to its orbit**, then **there would be no change of seasons**.
The amount of sunlight each hemisphere receives would be constant throughout the year, and there would be no solstices or equinoxes.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Which one of the following processes is not a part of long-wave radiation?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following processes is not a part of long-wave radiation?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Scattering** is the process by which particles or molecules in the atmosphere redirect the incoming solar radiation in different directions.
It occurs when light interacts with small particles or gas molecules and changes its direction. In the context of **long-wave radiation**, which refers to the emission of **infrared radiation** from the Earth's surface, scattering is not involved.
Long-wave radiation primarily involves the transfer of heat energy as electromagnetic waves with longer wavelengths. It occurs through the process of **radiation**, where the Earth's surface emits infrared radiation into the atmosphere. This emitted radiation can be absorbed or re-emitted by **greenhouse gases** in the atmosphere, contributing to the greenhouse effect.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: The lowest temperature is observed/ recorded in
which one of the following layers of the atmosphere?
questions.questionText: The lowest temperature is observed/ recorded in which one of the following layers of the atmosphere?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The lowest temperature is observed in the **mesosphere** of the atmosphere. The mesosphere is the third layer of the Earth's atmosphere, located above the stratosphere and below the thermosphere. As we move higher into the mesosphere, the **temperature decreases** with increasing altitude.
The coldest temperatures in the atmosphere, reaching as low as **- 90 degrees Celsius ( - 130 degrees Fahrenheit)**, are found in the mesosphere.
This layer is also known for its unique phenomena, such as the occurrence of **noctilucent clouds** and **meteors** burning up upon entry.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Presence of ozone in the atmosphere is important because it absorbs
questions.questionText: Presence of ozone in the atmosphere is important because it absorbs

options.d: Outgoing ultraviolet-B radiations and incoming ultraviolet-A radiations

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The **ozone layer** is a region of the upper atmosphere, between roughly 15 and 35 km above Earth's surface, containing relatively high concentrations of ozone molecules (O3).
The ozone layer effectively **blocks almost all solar radiation of wavelengths less than 290 nm**, which includes **UVC and most of UVB**. **Important Tips** The Sun emits different types of ultraviolet radiation, classified according to their wavelengths. The shortest wavelength is **ultraviolet-C (UVC)**, which ranges from 100 to 280 nanometers (nm).
The next is **ultraviolet-B (UVB)**, which ranges from 280 to 315 nm. The longest is **ultraviolet-A (UVA)**, which ranges from 315 to 400 nm.

statementLines: Which one of the following layers of atrnosphere has a high concentration of ions?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following layers of atrnosphere has a high concentration of ions?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The **thermosphere** is the layer of the atmosphere that is located above the mesosphere and below the exosphere.
It is the **hottest layer** of the atmosphere, and it has a **high concentration of ions**. The ions in the thermosphere are formed by the interaction of solar radiation with the atoms and molecules in the atmosphere.
**Important Tips Troposphere:** 0-12 km,

- 56 to-89 C. Coldest layer of atmosphere.
**Thermosphere:** 85-600 km,
- 89 to 1500 C. Ionosphere located in this layer.
**Exosphere:** 600-10000 km, **1500-10**000 C. Very thin layer of atmosphere, no definite upper limit.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Which of the following statements relating to the solar system is/are correct?
1. Venus and Uranus rotate opposite to the direction of rotation of Earth.
2. Venus is the nearest planet to Earth.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements relating to the solar system is/are correct? 1. Venus and Uranus rotate opposite to the direction of rotation of Earth. 2. Venus is the nearest planet to Earth. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is correct -**Venus and Uranus**are the only two planets in the solar system that**rotate opposite to the direction of rotation of Earth**.**
Venus rotates clockwise, while Earth rotates counterclockwise. Uranus rotates on its side, and its rotation is also retrograde.

**Statement 2 is correct -**Venus is the second planet from the Sun**, and it is the**closest planet to Earth**.**
The average distance between Venus and Earth is about 41 million kilometers. **Important Tips** Uranus, with an orbital period of 84 years, was discovered by William Herschel and has a total of 27 moons.
On the other hand, Venus has an orbital period of 225 days and no confirmed moons. It was Galileo Galilei who made the first accurate observation of Venus in 1610.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Pressure, Winds, Airmasses (Coriolis Force, Frictional Force, etc.)
statementLines: Intertropkal Convergence Zone (ITCZ), a low-pressure zone, is located:
questions.questionText: Intertropkal Convergence Zone (ITCZ), a low-pressure zone, is located:

options.a: Between trade winds and westerlies belt
options.b: Between westerlies and polar winds belt

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **Intertropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ)** is a low-pressure zone **located at the equator**.
It is the area where the northeast and southeast trade winds converge. Sailors often refer to it as the '**doldrums**' due to its windless conditions.
The ITCZ encircles the Earth near the thermal equator but can vary in its specific position seasonally. It is a significant meteorological feature that influences weather patterns and precipitation in tropical regions.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: India was the part of an dent Gondwana land Supercontinent. H includes the following landmass of the present:
questions.questionText: India was the part of an dent Gondwana land Supercontinent. H includes the following landmass of the present:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. India was part of the ancient **Gondwana supercontinent**. The Gondwana supercontinent included **South America, Africa, Arabia, Madagascar, India, Australia, and Antarctica**.
It was a vast landmass that existed around 600 million years ago and started breaking apart during the Early Jurassic Period, about 180 million years ago, leading to the formation of the continents we recognize today.
**Important Tips** The assembly and breakup of Gondwana: Gondwana was assembled through continental collisions during the Late Precambrian, roughly 1 billion to 542 million years ago.
It eventually collided with other landmasses, including North America, Europe, and Siberia, forming the supercontinent of Pangea. The breakup of Gondwana occurred in stages, primarily during the Mesozoic Era. Around 180 million years ago, during the Jurassic Period, the western half of Gondwana (comprising Africannd South America) began to separate from the eastern half (including Madagascar, India, Australia, and Antarctica).
This separation led to the opening of the South Atlantic Ocean about 140 million years ago as Africa separated from South America. Around the same time, India, still attached to Madagascar, started to separate from Antarcticannd Australia, initiating the formation of the central Indian Ocean.
During the Late Cretaceous Period, about 50 million years ago, India finally broke away from Madagascar. Australia also gradually rifted away from Antarctica.
India eventually collided with Eurasia, forming the Himalayan Mountain range. The northward movement of the Australian plate began its collision along the southern margin of Southeast Asia, an ongoing process.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: The "Sea of Tranquility" Hes on-
questions.questionText: The "Sea of Tranquility" Hes on-
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **Sea of Tranquility** is a dark spot located in the northern hemisphere of the **Moon**.
It is not a body of water, but a lower altitude plain filled with dark, solidified lava. It is also the first location on another celestial body to be visited by humans, as the Apollo 11 mission landed there in **1969**.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: "Light Year" is the unit of-
questions.questionText: "Light Year" is the unit of-
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. A **light-year is a unit of length** used to express astronomical distances and is equivalent to about 9.46 trillion kilometers (9.46 x 10^12 km), or 5.88 trillion miles (5.88 x 10^12 mi).
As defined by the International Astronomical Union (IAU), a light-year is the **distance that light travels in a vacuum in one Julian year** (365.25 days).
A light-year is not a unit of time, as the name might imply. A light-year is most often used when expressing distances to stars and other distances on a galactic scale, especially in non-specialist contexts and popular science publications.
The unit most commonly used in professional astronomy is the parsec (symbol: pc, about 3.26 light-years) which derives from astrometry; it is the distance at which one astronomical unit (au) subtends an angle of one second of arc.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: The earth is nearest to the sun on-
questions.questionText: The earth is nearest to the sun on-
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The Earth is closest to the Sun, or at its **perihelion**, about two weeks after the December solstice, when it is winter in the Northern Hemisphere.
The date of the perihelion varies slightly from year to year, but it usually occurs around **January 3 or 4**. On January 4, **2023**, the Earth was about 147.1 million km (91.4 million mi) away from the Sun.
This is about 5 million km (3 million mi) closer than the average distance between the Earth and the Sun, which is about 150 million km (93 million mi).
However, the Earth's distance from the Sun does not cause seasons or affect the global temperature significantly. Seasons are mostly caused by the tilt of the Earth's axis, which affects the amount of sunlight each hemisphere receives.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Zero-degree latitude and zero-degree longitude Hes in the-
questions.questionText: Zero-degree latitude and zero-degree longitude Hes in the-
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The intersection of zero-degree latitude (the Equator) and zero-degree longitude (the Prime Meridian) is located in the **Atlantic Ocean**, specifically in the Gulf of Guinea off the coast of western Africa.
This point serves as a reference for global navigation and is where the coordinates (0N 0E) meet. **Important Tips** The closest country to 0, 0 is the African country of Ghana which lies about 614 km (382 mi) to the north of this confluence.
The closest piece of land to 0, 0 is a small islet offshore of Ghana, between Akwidaa and Dixcove at the latitude and longitude coordinates of 445'30"N, 158'33"W.
The distance from this islet to 0, 0 is about 570 km (354.2 mi). **Concept of Null Island:** Null Island is a fictional geographic location situated at the precise intersection of zero degrees latitude (the Equator) and zero degrees longitude (the Prime Meridian).
It gained recognition with the release of publicly available Natural Earth GIS data. This one-square-meter "island" serves as a troubleshooting tool for identifying geocoding failures, where geographic data cannot be accurately located due to errors.
In cases where GIS software or mapping tools struggle to assign coordinates to an address, the record may be temporarily assigned a coordinate of 0,0 at Null Island to signal a potential issue in the data.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: When the length of day and night is equal the Sunrays falls perpendicular at-
questions.questionText: When the length of day and night is equal the Sunrays falls perpendicular at-
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The equinoxes are the only times of the year when the **subsolar point is directly on the Equator**.
The subsolar point is an area where the sun's rays shine perpendicular to Earth's surface"a right angle. This means that the **length of day and night is equal** for both hemispheres on the equinoxes.
The equinoxes occur around March 21 and September 22 every year.

statementLines: The causes of Day and Night is-
questions.questionText: The causes of Day and Night is-

options.b: Rotation of Earth on its axis

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. Day and night are caused by the **rotation of the Earth on its axis**, which makes different parts of the planet face the Sun or away from it at different times.
The Earth rotates once every 24 hours, which means that every part of the planet experiences a period of daylight and a period of darkness in that time frame.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: The latitude at which 'Annual Range of Temperature' remains minimum is-
questions.questionText: The latitude at which 'Annual Range of Temperature' remains minimum is-
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The latitude at which the annual range of temperature remains minimum is the **Equator**. At the Equator, the Sun's rays strike almost vertically year-round, resulting in consistent and nearly equal day and night lengths throughout the year.
This near-vertical angle of sunlight leads to **minimal variation in temperature between seasons**. Consequently, the Equator experiences a relatively stable and warm climate with a minimal annual temperature range compared to regions at higher latitudes.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: If the distance between the sun and the earth is reduced by quarter to present distance, then probability of which one will increase-
questions.questionText: If the distance between the sun and the earth is reduced by quarter to present distance, then probability of which one will increase-

options.a: Earth would fall into the Sun.
options.c: The duration of our year will be decreased.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. If the distance between the sun and the earth is reduced by a quarter to the present distance, then the **duration of our year will be decreased**.
This is because the orbital period of a planet depends on its distance from the Sun, according to **Kepler's third law of planetary motion**.
The law states that the square of the orbital period is proportional to the cube of the semi-major axis of the orbit. If the distance between the Sun and the Earth is reduced by a quarter, then the semi-major axis of the Earth's orbit will also be reduced by a quarter.
This means that the orbital period will be reduced by a factor of 0.5" (3/2), which is approximately 0.35. Therefore, the duration of our year will be decreased by 65%.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Seasonal changes are caused by which one of the special characteristics of Earth's motion?
questions.questionText: Seasonal changes are caused by which one of the special characteristics of Earth's motion?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. Seasonal changes are caused by both the **revolution of Earth around the Sun** and the **axial tilt of Earth by 23.5 degrees**.
The revolution of Earth around the Sun determines the length of the year and the seasons, while the axial tilt of Earth determines how different parts of Earth receive more or less solar energy at different times of the year.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Which of the following does not belong to the solar system?
questions.questionText: Which of the following does not belong to the solar system?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. A **nebula is not a part of the solar system**, but a giant cloud of dust and gas in interstellar space.
A nebula can be the result of a dying star, such as a supernova, or a region where new stars are forming. Nebulae are very far away from the Earth and can only be seen with powerful telescopes.
**Important Tips Asteroids** are rocky objects that orbit the Sun, mostly between Mars and Jupiter. **Comets** are icy bodies that have highly elliptical orbits around the Sun and develop tails when they approach it.
**Planets** are large spherical objects that orbit the Sun and clear their vicinity of other smaller objects. The **solar system consists of eight planets**: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus and Neptune.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Interior of Earth and Continental Theories
statementLines: Continents have drifted apart because of-
questions.questionText: Continents have drifted apart because of-
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. Continents have drifted apart because of **tectonic activities**, which are the movements of the Earth's tectonic plates.
Tectonic plates are large slabs of rock that float on the Earth's mantle. They are constantly moving, and their movements can cause continents to drift apart, collide, or slide past each other.
Tectonic activities are caused by the **heat that is generated in the Earth's core**. This heat causes the mantle to flow, which in turn moves the tectonic plates.
The theory of continental drift, which was first proposed by **Alfred Wegener in 1912**, states that the continents were once all joined together in a single supercontinent called **Pangaea**.
Over time, Pangaea broke apart and the continents drifted to their current positions.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Which planet is known as the 'Evening Star'?
questions.questionText: Which planet is known as the 'Evening Star'?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Venus** is the planet that is most often called the 'Evening Star', because it is the brightest object in the night sky after the Moon and the Sun, and it usually appears in the western sky after sunset.
Venus is also sometimes called the 'Morning Star', because it can also appear in the eastern sky before sunrise. Whether Venus is an evening star, or a morning star depends on its position relative to the Earth and the Sun.
When Venus is on the same side of the Sun as the Earth, it is a morning star; when it is on the opposite side of the Sun, it is an evening star.
Venus alternates between being an evening star and a morning star every 584 days, or about 19 months. **Important Tips** Facts on Venus Planet:
Venus has a thick atmosphere that consists mainly of **carbon dioxide and sulfuric acid clouds**. The atmosphere traps heat and creates a runaway greenhouse effect, making Venus the hottest planet in our solar system.
Venus also **rotates in the opposite direction** of most other planets, including Earth. This means that the Sun rises in the west and sets in the east on Venus.
Venus rotates very slowly, taking 243 Earth days to complete one spin. This makes a **day on Venus longer than a year on Venus**, which lasts 225 Earth days.

Ganymede, satellite of Saturn, is the largest satellite in the solar system III. Neptune is surrounded by methane gas rings of sub-zero temperature IV. Phobos and Deimos are two satellites of Mars Code:

options.a: Only I and II are correct
options.b: Only II and III are correct
options.c: Only III and IV are correct
options.d: I, II, III and IV all are correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer.

**Statement I is incorrect: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun, but it is not the hottest.** Mercury has a very thin atmosphere that cannot trap heat, so its surface temperature varies greatly from day to night.
The **hottest planet in the solar system is Venus**, which has a thick atmosphere that creates a greenhouse effect and keeps the surface temperature at a constant 462 C (864 F).
**Statement II is incorrect:**Ganymede**is the largest satellite in the solar system, but it is not a satellite of Saturn.** Ganymede is a moon of Jupiter, and it is larger than Mercury and Pluto, and three-quarters the size of Mars.
**Statement III is correct:**Neptune has six rings**that are composed mainly of dust and ice particles, some of which contain**methane gas**.** The rings are very faint and hard to see, and they orbit at a very cold distance from the Sun, about 30 times farther than Earth.
The average temperature on Neptune is about-200 C (
- 328 F).
**Statement IV is correct:**Phobos and Deimos are the two moons of Mars**, and they are named after the mythological twin characters Phobos (fear and panic) and Deimos (terror and dread) who accompanied their father Ares (Mars) into battle.** Both Phobos and Deimos were discovered in 1877 by American astronomer Asaph Hall.
They are irregular in shape and have surface materials similar to many asteroids in the outer asteroid belt, which suggests that they are captured asteroids.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Which of the following phenomenon happens in India when the sun-shines vertically over the Tropic of Capricorn?
questions.questionText: Which of the following phenomenon happens in India when the sun-shines vertically over the Tropic of Capricorn?

options.a: High pressure develops over North-West India
options.b: Low pressure develops over North-West India
options.c: No change in pressure occurs over North West India

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. When the sun shines vertically over the Tropic of Capricorn, it is summer in the Southern Hemisphere and winter in the Northern Hemisphere.
This means that the air in **North-West India is cooler** than the air in the surrounding regions. As a result, the air in North-West India contracts and sinks, creating a **high-pressure area**.
**Important Tips** This high-pressure area is known as the **subtropical high pressure belt or the anticyclone**. It is responsible for the clear skies and dry weather that are typically experienced in North-West India during the winter months.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Which of the following statements is true regarding earth movement?
1. Earth rotates on its axis from west to east resulting in seasonal changes.
2. Earth revolution around sun results in occurrence of day and night on earth.
Code:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is true regarding earth movement? 1. Earth rotates on its axis from west to east resulting in seasonal changes. 2. Earth revolution around sun results in occurrence of day and night on earth. Code:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. Statement 1 is false: **Earth's rotation on its axis does not cause seasonal changes**, but it causes the **day and night cycle** on Earth.
Earth rotates on its axis once every 24 hours, making one complete turn from west to east. This means that different parts of Earth face the Sun at different times of the day, creating periods of light and darkness.
Earth's rotation also affects the apparent movement of the Sun, the Moon, and the stars across the sky, as well as the Coriolis effect, which influences the wind and ocean currents.
Statement 2 is false: **Earth's revolution around the Sun does not cause the occurrence of day and night** on Earth, but it causes **seasonal changes** and variations in day length throughout the year.
Earth revolves around the Sun in an elliptical orbit once every 365.25 days, or one year. This means that Earth's distance from the Sun changes slightly during its orbit, but this does not affect the global temperature significantly.
What causes seasonal changes is **Earth's axial tilt**, which is about 23.5 degrees from the vertical. This means that Earth's axis always points in the same direction in space, regardless of its position in its orbit.
As a result, different parts of Earth receive different amounts of sunlight and heat throughout the year, creating seasons.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Equinox, the two periods in the year when day and night are equal, occurs on:
questions.questionText: Equinox, the two periods in the year when day and night are equal, occurs on:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. Equinoxes, the two periods in the year when day and night are nearly equal in duration, occur on **21 March (Vernal equinox)** and **23 September (Autumnal equinox)**.
These equinoxes mark the times when the Earth's axis is neither tilted toward nor away from the Sun, resulting in nearly equal hours of daylight and darkness at all latitudes.
The term "equinox" comes from the Latin words "aequus" (equal) and "nox" (night), reflecting the balanced distribution of daylight and nighttime during these periods.
**Important Tips** Equinoxes mark the transition between seasons, with the March equinox marking the start of spring (in the Northern Hemisphere) and the September equinox marking the start of autumn (in the Northern Hemisphere).
During equinoxes the Sun crosses the Earth's equator, and it appears to rise "due east" and set "due west" on the equator. This happens because the plane of Earth's equator aligns with the center of the Sun's disk. The "nearly" equal hours of day and night are due to **refraction of sunlight** or a bending of the light's rays that causes the sun to appear above the horizon when the actual position of the sun is below the horizon.
Additionally, the days become a little longer at the higher latitudes (those at a distance from the equator) because it takes the sun longer to rise and set.
Therefore, on the equinox and for several days before and after the equinox, the length of day will range from about 12 hours and six and one-half minutes at the equator, to 12 hours and 8 minutes at 30 degrees latitude, to 12 hours and 16 minutes at 60 degrees latitude.
